free_text: Analysis of the 12C(p(pol),p)12C reaction at 200 MeV
in the distorted-wave impulse approximation
Polarized proton beams with energies of 200.1
and 199.8 MeV.  Beam polarization typically 70%.
Two natural carbon targets with thicknesses of 3.74+-
Two sets of measurements.  More than one value given
at some angles.
Beam polarization determined by helium elastic
scattering asymmetry measurements.
Quadrupole-dipole-dipole-multipole magnetic
spectrometer.
Helical-cathode position-sensitive proportional
chamber used as focal plane detector.
Two plastic scintillator detectors 0.63 and 1.27 cm
thick, placed behind the chamber, used for particle
indentification.
Faraday cup internal to the scattering chamber used
for angles <23 degrees.
External Faraday cup used for angles >23 degrees.
Relative scattering angle believed correct to 0.03
degrees.  Absolute angle error <0.1 degrees.
Data received by email, J. Comfort, 2 November 1999.
No reply from J. Comfort to author proof.
Converted from O0207 (CaJaD); BIB updated,
authors data added (VM).
Mainly statistical uncertainty, however, a
5% error added in quadrature to the statistical
uncertainty for data <23 degrees, since internal
Faraday cup is believed to have failed to collect
about 1/3 of total beam charge.
Relative cross sections believed to be correct to
<3%. Uncertainty in absolute cross section scale
estimated <10%.
Uncertainties given take into account all
known sources of error.
